claim: For all integers n >= 1, the exponent of 2 in (2n)! is at least n.
predicate: vp(math.factorial(2*n), 2) >= n

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the exponent of 2 in (2n)! is strictly greater than n when n is odd.
predicate: n % 2 == 1 and vp(math.factorial(2*n), 2) > n

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the exponent of 2 in (2n)! is at least n+1 when n is a power of 2.
predicate: n > 0 and (n & (n-1)) == 0 and vp(math.factorial(2*n), 2) >= n+1

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is odd, then (2n)! is divisible by 2^{n+1} but not by 2^{n+2} for n=3,7,15.
predicate: n % 2 == 1 and (n in [3,7,15]) and vp(math.factorial(2*n), 2) == n+1

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a power of 2, then (2n)! is divisible by 2^{n+2} when n >= 4.

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the exponent of 2 in (2n)! minus n is even.
predicate: (vp(math.factorial(2*n), 2) - n) % 2 == 0

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is even, then the exponent of 2 in (2n)! minus n is divisible by 4.

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is odd, then (2n)! / 2^n is odd only when n=1.
